user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
bcs this means that customers heavily sold downside gammas, the MM speed profile was very steep.
this means two things:
1) the customers were not afraid of selling those puts,
2) dealers are longer gamma downward, so whenever the spot falls, they will be forced to buy more and more assets against the spot movement, providing an opposite side against the sellers

comment.body:
so customer interest was to sell downside volatility, 
and by that they artificially/mechanically increased the downside liquidity... this is why i said "leveraged liquidity", bcs the customers increased their 'strenght' using market makers' hedging flows

comment.body:
as I said,
gamma is realized volatility

when you sell gamma, you sell realized volatility, and vice versa.

and so speed (the change of gamma relative to spot) is like the vanna of RV

user.name: Alma

comment.body: why so?
comment.raw_body: why so?

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
bcs you can be either net
1) sell OTM puts, buy OTM calls
or
2) buy OTM puts and sell OTM calls

in the first case you short the skew, so market makers will long the skew, (bcs skew is 22d put IV minus 22d call IV per ATM IV) and you generate a negative speed profile.
this means, that the artificial liquidity is pushed downward,
while illiquidity (vacuum) expectations are to the upside.

This pushes the spot/rv correlation upward, meaning you expect a right skewed distribution: lower vol downward, higher vol upward
comment.raw_body:
bcs you can be either net

or
2) buy OTM puts and sell OTM calls

in the first case you short the skew, so market makers will long the skew, (bcs skew is 22d put IV minus 22d call IV per ATM IV) and you generate a negative speed profile.
this means, that the artificial liquidity is pushed downward,
while illiquidity (vacuum) expectations are to the upside.

This pushes the spot/rv correlation upward, meaning you expect a right skewed distribution: lower vol downward, higher vol upward

comment.body:
so, this is the true meaning/significance of knowing dealer positioning.

why do I prefer this over simply volume profiles? bcs 1) once i have the opportunity to have an assumption-free model of the structure,why not to use it? 2) it is literally what the market expects. I don't need to assume anything.
still one must watch the tendencies, and volume profile... bcs expected and realized distribution are two different things!
